phrasalVerbs.definition: To stop eating something or doing something, especially because it is bad for your health.
examples: The doctor advised me to cut out sugar completely.
She's trying to cut out caffeine from her diet.
You should cut out smoking if you want to feel healthier.

phrasalVerbs.definition: To disappear slowly; to gradually become less distinct.
examples: The music faded away as we walked further from the party.
Her enthusiasm faded away once she realised the difficulty.
Old memories tend to fade away over time.

phrasalVerbs.definition: To make someone feel sad or lose hope.
examples: The constant rain is really getting me down.
Don't let negative comments get you down.
The lack of progress was getting the whole team down.

phrasalVerbs.definition: To stop liking someone or something.
examples: I've gone off coffee lately—it tastes too bitter.
She went off him after discovering his true character.
Many people go off their favourite foods during pregnancy.

phrasalVerbs.definition: To give something a more interesting quality; to make something more exciting.
examples: Some music would liven up this party.
He told jokes to liven up the presentation.
The new artwork really livened up the office space.

phrasalVerbs.definition: To manage to produce a quality or a reaction that helps you deal with a difficult situation.
examples: She summoned up the courage to speak in front of the crowd.
He tried to summon up enthusiasm for the project.
I couldn't summon up the energy to go to the gym.

phrasalVerbs.definition: To spend time in a relaxed way when you have nothing else to do.
examples: We whiled away the afternoon playing chess.
She whiled away the hours reading novels on the beach.
They whiled away the evening with pleasant conversation.

fillInExercises.sentence: The doctor recommended that he ___ processed foods.
fillInExercises.explanation: 'Cut out' means to stop consuming something unhealthy.

fillInExercises.sentence: The sound of the train slowly ___ away into the distance.
The gloomy weather has really been ___ me ___.
fillInExercises.explanation: 'Get down' means to make someone feel sad or depressed.
'Go off' means to stop liking something.

fillInExercises.sentence: Let's add some decorations to ___ this room.
fillInExercises.explanation: 'Liven up' means to make something more interesting or exciting.

fillInExercises.sentence: She had to ___ up all her strength to complete the marathon.
fillInExercises.explanation: 'Summon up' means to gather courage or strength.

fillInExercises.sentence: They ___ the afternoon at the café, chatting and people-watching.
fillInExercises.explanation: 'While away' means to spend time in a relaxed, leisurely manner.

fillInExercises.sentence: Some music will really ___ up this dull party.
fillInExercises.explanation: 'Liven up' means to make something more energetic or exciting.

explanationContent.sentenceBreakdown: I've decided to cut out junk food from my diet.
explanationContent.commonMistake: Do not confuse this with other items from the lesson — here you need *cut out* (*cut out*).
